opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My head feels like it's going to physically explode, and there are blurry grey spots all over my vision.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The headache started about 6 hours ago and just keeps getting worse, and now my home blood pressure machine keeps reading really high. My spouse says I'm getting confused.

symptom_narrative.character: a severe bursting headache, like my head is going to explode
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst headache I've ever had, I can't move because of it
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and getting worse the whole time
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps building on its own; bright light makes it worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: big blurry grey blind spots in both eyes, feeling a bit short of breath, I threw up once, and I feel dizzy and slowed down
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: sitting slumped on the couch, can't get up because of the headache and dizziness

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: spouse is present and very worried; patient stopped his blood pressure tablets two weeks ago because he felt fine

objective_data_offered.value: over 210/120 mmHg, repeated readings above 200 systolic
objective_data_offered.timing: over the last hour at home
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-measured on a home blood pressure machine; readings this extreme should be confirmed but are highly concerning even if the cuff is imprecise

objective_data_offered.value: spouse says he has become increasingly lethargic and confused over the past 2 hours and keeps repeating the same questions
spouse says he looks flushed and distressed, is slumped on the couch rubbing his temples, and vomited once into a bowl beside him
